rawIntent: The CI workflow does not trigger on push to gestalt/** branches. The workflow file at .github/workflows/ci.yml is missing or does not contain the correct on.push.branches filter. The deploy orchestrator reported: 'GitHub Actions: no push-triggered run appeared on branch gestalt/2a22ec77-feature-add-a-status-module within 21s. Verify the workflow file has `on: push: branches: ['gestalt/**']` and that pr-agent's push actually landed.' This prevents automated CI from running on feature branches. Preserve all existing exports, types, interfaces, and imports. Only add or modify what is needed to resolve the CI failure shown above.

successCriteria.description: A CI workflow file exists at .github/workflows/ci.yml that triggers on push to branches matching the pattern 'gestalt/**'
The workflow runs the project's build and test commands as defined in HARNESS.json (npm run build && npx jest --passWithNoTests) so that CI gates pass on gestalt feature branches

constraints: Only add or modify .github/workflows/ci.yml — do not touch any application source files, domain modules, types, interfaces, or imports

The workflow must use the test command from HARNESS.json codeGeneration.verification.testCmd: 'find src -name '*.ts' -not -name '*.spec.ts' -not -name '*.test.ts' -not -name '*.d.ts' -type f | grep -q . && npm run build && npx jest --passWithNoTests'
No @gestalt/* packages in any generated files
outOfScope: All application source code under src/
All domain modules (leave, balance, employee, policy, notification, audit, status)
All shared types, DTOs, interfaces, repositories, services, controllers, routes
Any package.json, tsconfig.json, or other project configuration files

ambiguities.description: The intent says the file is 'missing or does not contain the correct on.push.branches filter.' The file does not exist in the repository. It is unclear whether the CI workflow previously existed and was deleted, or was never created. If it existed, we may need to restore its full content (jobs, steps, runners, etc.) rather than just the trigger. If it never existed, we need to create a minimal but complete CI workflow from scratch.
options: Create a minimal new ci.yml with only the correct trigger and the build+test commands from HARNESS.json
Restore a presumed-lost ci.yml with full CI steps (lint, typecheck, unit tests, Semgrep security pass) as described in HARNESS.json description

ambiguities.description: The intent mentions 'Preserve all existing exports, types, interfaces, and imports' — language typically used for source code changes, not CI config. This may indicate the intent was templated from a code-change intent and the preservation clause is vestigial, or it may hint that the ci.yml previously referenced specific source files.
options: The preservation clause is vestigial and can be ignored for a CI config change
The preservation clause implies the ci.yml existed and contained references to source artifacts that must be preserved
